pages.title: Erratum for SGA 4, volume 3
pages.html:
Erratum for SGA 4, volume 3

XIV p.18 1.14

(XIX 6) instead of (XX 6).

XVI 2.2

It is necessary to assume \(F\) locally constant!

XVI 5.2

The demonstration given is incomplete. After the statement of the hypothesis of recurrence, it is necessary first to look back to the case where \(F\) is constant (\(F\) becomes constant on a galoisian covering \(\pi:X'\mathchoice{\longrightarrow}{\rightarrow}{\rightarrow}{\rightarrow} X\) of \(X\), of Galois group \(G\), and the spectral continuation of Hochschild-Serre \(E_2^{p q}=\underline{\operatorname{H}}^p(G,\underline{\operatorname{H}}^q(X',\pi^\ast F))\Rightarrow \underline{\operatorname{H}}^{p+q}(X,F)\) is invoked). The following arguments are then correct.

XVII 1.1.8

The sign (1.1.8.1) is wrong when \(F\) is contravariant in some variables.

\[ \tag{1.1.8.1} \rho^{\underline k} = (-1)^{A(\underline k)}: \text{ automorphism of } F\circ (G_j)\left(K_i^{k_i\varepsilon(i)\varepsilon(\psi(i))}\right) \]

with

\[ A(\underline k) = \sum_{\varepsilon(i) = \varepsilon(\psi(i))=-} k_i + \sum_{\varepsilon(j)=-} \sum_{\substack{\psi(a)=\psi(b)=j \\ a<b}} k_a k_b \]

XVII 2.1.3

The demonstration contains gross errors. The 3-th line (p.34 1.9) should be deleted and the 6th and 7th (p.34 1.12, 13) replaced by:

The arrows in the diagram (2.1.3.2) induce applications

\[ \hom_y(F,F) \mathchoice{\longrightarrow}{\rightarrow}{\rightarrow}{\rightarrow} \hom_{f g'}({g'}^\ast F,f_\ast F) = \hom_{x'}(f'_\ast {g'}^\ast F,g^\ast f_\ast F) \text{,} \]

XVIII 2.14.4

Read XVII 6.2.7.2 instead of XVII 6.2.4.

XVIII p.99 1-1

Read \(u\) instead of \(U\) and 3.1.16.1 instead of 3.1.11.1.

blocks.html: It is necessary to assume \(F\) locally constant!

The demonstration given is incomplete. After the statement of the hypothesis of recurrence, it is necessary first to look back to the case where \(F\) is constant (\(F\) becomes constant on a galoisian covering \(\pi:X'\mathchoice{\longrightarrow}{\rightarrow}{\rightarrow}{\rightarrow} X\) of \(X\), of Galois group \(G\), and the spectral continuation of Hochschild-Serre \(E_2^{p q}=\underline{\operatorname{H}}^p(G,\underline{\operatorname{H}}^q(X',\pi^\ast F))\Rightarrow \underline{\operatorname{H}}^{p+q}(X,F)\) is invoked). The following arguments are then correct.
The sign (1.1.8.1) is wrong when \(F\) is contravariant in some variables.
\[ \tag{1.1.8.1} \rho^{\underline k} = (-1)^{A(\underline k)}: \text{ automorphism of } F\circ (G_j)\left(K_i^{k_i\varepsilon(i)\varepsilon(\psi(i))}\right) \]
\[ A(\underline k) = \sum_{\varepsilon(i) = \varepsilon(\psi(i))=-} k_i + \sum_{\varepsilon(j)=-} \sum_{\substack{\psi(a)=\psi(b)=j \\ a<b}} k_a k_b \]
The demonstration contains gross errors. The 3-th line (p.34 1.9) should be deleted and the 6th and 7th (p.34 1.12, 13) replaced by:
The arrows in the diagram (2.1.3.2) induce applications
\[ \hom_y(F,F) \mathchoice{\longrightarrow}{\rightarrow}{\rightarrow}{\rightarrow} \hom_{f g'}({g'}^\ast F,f_\ast F) = \hom_{x'}(f'_\ast {g'}^\ast F,g^\ast f_\ast F) \text{,} \]
